message:
perf(fr-runtime): lazy threat-event digests and ACL category short-circuit

Two profile-driven optimizations that moved FrankenRedis from ~1.3% to
79-99% of Redis throughput on per-command workloads:

1. Lazy digest computation: replace eager input_digest and state_before
   calls (which ran on every command) with a ThreatInputDigestSource enum
   that only materializes digests when a threat event actually fires. The
   success path now pays zero digest cost.

2. ACL category short-circuit: when a user has no category-level ACL
   rules (the common case for the default +@all user), skip the
   command_categories scan entirely. Profiling showed ~71% of CPU was
   spent on this lookup before the short-circuit.

Also refactors ThreatEventInput to carry an input_source variant instead
of a pre-computed input_digest + state_before pair, and introduces
argv_to_resp_bytes() for canonical RESP encoding of parsed argv lists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
